marketState: neutral, slight bearish tilt — storage surplus and bearish technical trend structure offset by hot near-term weather demand
summary: NG front month ($2.940) is sitting right at modeled fair value ($2.917 now, FAIR status at all four horizons out to +21D), so there is no clear mispricing edge to trade off the fair-value band alone. Seasonality is weak evidence here — reliability is flagged 'Low' (50pp spread) — but it tilts bearish: price is -10.3pp below its typical-year median, odds of being higher in 1/2/4 weeks are only 38%/38%/31%, and the typical 4-week move is -3.1%. Storage is the dominant bearish fundamental: +184 Bcf above the 5-year average with a much larger-than-forecast build (+72 Bcf bearish surprise). Near-term weather is a real bullish counterweight — demand is running +14.7% above the 5-year norm over the next 7 days near the top of its range (0.86) — but that heat fades into the 8-16 day window (+7%, rangePos 0.64). Technicals show a broad bearish trend (50<200 EMA cross on every timeframe from 15m to 1w) yet the 4h/1d are oversold (RSI 24.9/35.6, price pierced below the lower daily Bollinger band), which argues for a possible short-term bounce even while the larger trend stays down.

longTerm.rationale: Storage is +184 Bcf above the 5-year average with a bearish +72 Bcf build surprise, managed money is already net short (-60,295 contracts, ratio 0.8) though only at the 35th percentile of its 5y range, and every technical timeframe from 15m to 1w shows a bearish 50<200 EMA structure. Seasonality adds a mild bearish lean (price -10.3pp below median, typical 4-week move -3.1%) even though its reliability is flagged Low, and price is trading right at (not below) fair value so there's little valuation support for a bounce to persist.
longTerm.invalidation: Sustained close above ~$3.15-3.20 (upper-mid fair value band), or a storage deficit emerging vs 5y, or the 8-16D weather outlook re-accelerating well above +14% vs 5y

shortTerm.sizing: 1R approx 1% account risk (countertrend bounce — keep size conservative)
shortTerm.rationale: Next-7-day demand is running +14.7% above the 5-year norm near the top of its range (0.86), the front-Sep spread shows slight backwardation (-$0.045) consistent with near-term tightness, and price sits right at fair value with no valuation headwind. The 4h RSI (24.9) and daily close below the lower Bollinger band point to a short-term oversold condition ripe for a mean-reversion bounce, even though the dominant multi-timeframe trend remains bearish.
shortTerm.invalidation: Close back below $2.80 without a bounce, or 1-7D weather demand rolling over toward/below the 5y norm

keyDrivers: Storage +184 Bcf above 5y average with a bearish +72 Bcf build surprise vs forecast
Near-term weather demand +14.7% vs 5y (rangePos 0.86) fading to +7% (rangePos 0.64) by 8-16D
Price at fair value (FAIR status) across all horizons, with modeled fair value rising from $2.917 to $3.019 over 21 days
Managed money net short -60,295 contracts (ratio 0.8), 35th percentile of 5y range
Bearish 50<200 EMA trend structure on every technical timeframe, but 4h/1d oversold (RSI 24.9/35.6, daily below lower Bollinger band)
risks: Seasonality reliability is explicitly 'Low' (50pp spread) — low confidence in the seasonal probability and typical-move signals
Weather forecasts can swing quickly; a hotter-than-expected 8-16D outlook could flip the demand picture bullish
